paper_title: Experimental Methods for Pollen Grain Imaging and Analysis

methods.description: This method involves the optical imaging and laser scattering of pollen grains recorded in parallel using the same setup. It is used to train and test a neural network for generating images of unseen pollen grains and species.

steps.description: Procure Iva xanthiifolia and Populus deltoides pollen grains from Sigma Aldrich, and collect Narcissus and Mahonia aquifolium pollen grains from the University of Southampton grounds.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain pollen grains from specified sources

steps.description: Deposit pollen grains onto a substrate (a 25 mm by 75 mm, 1 mm thick soda-lime glass slide).
steps.searchable_summary: place pollen grains on glass slide

steps.description: Individually locate 120 pollen grains and record scattering patterns and optical images for each.
steps.searchable_summary: record scattering patterns and images of pollen grains

steps.description: Record images and scattering patterns for Mahonia aquifolium pollen to test the neural network's capability.
steps.searchable_summary: test neural network with Mahonia aquifolium pollen

methods.description: This method involves imaging pollen grains using different apparatus such as a visible light microscope and SEM, to obtain additional imaging data for analysis.

steps.description: Obtain additional pollen grains from purchased flowers and deposit them on a separate glass slide.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and prepare additional pollen samples

steps.description: Record scattering patterns and optical images for 100 different pollen grains using the in situ setup.
steps.searchable_summary: capture scattering patterns and images of additional pollen

steps.description: Perform z-stack optical microscopy and combine images via maximum intensity projection.
steps.searchable_summary: perform z-stack microscopy and image projection

steps.description: Carry out scanning electron microscopy (SEM) imaging after coating samples with Au/Pd.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SEM imaging on coated samples

methods.description: This setup involves using laser diodes and a CMOS camera to capture forward scattered light from pollen grains for imaging.

steps.description: Focus light from 3 laser diodes on the surface of a pollen-coated glass slide.
steps.searchable_summary: focus laser light on pollen slide

steps.description: Collect forward scattered light using a CMOS colour camera.
steps.searchable_summary: capture scattered light with CMOS camera

steps.description: Illuminate pollen grains with a white light source and image using a beam splitter and objective.
steps.searchable_summary: illuminate and image pollen with white light

methods.description: This setup involves using z-stack optical microscopy and SEM for detailed imaging of pollen grains.

steps.description: Perform z-stack optical microscopy using a Nikon microscope and CMOS camera.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct z-stack microscopy with Nikon setup

steps.description: Combine z-stack images via maximum intensity projection.
steps.searchable_summary: apply maximum intensity projection to z-stack

steps.description: Perform SEM imaging with a Zeiss Evo SEM after coating samples with Au/Pd.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct SEM imaging on Au/Pd coated samples
